header_0.cue: A coworker takes credit for your idea
header_0.raw_response: In this situation, I would place the marker at 60. While I value maintaining a harmonious work environment, I also believe in standing up for my work and ideas. I would first approach the coworker privately to discuss the issue, trying to resolve it without causing a scene. However, if the behavior continues, I would not hesitate to involve our manager or HR to ensure my contributions are recognized.

header_2.raw_response: Planning a weekend trip falls more on the 'Detailed itinerary' side of the spectrum for me. While I appreciate some spontaneity, I also like to have a general idea of what I want to see and do. Having a loose plan allows me to make the most of my time without feeling too restricted or stressed about making decisions on the spot. For example, when John and I went to Yosemite, we had a rough plan of where we wanted to go and what we wanted to see, but we also left room for exploring and changing our plans based on how we felt.
